questions.text: Which subject do you find most enjoyable and easiest to understand?
options: Mathematics and Science
Business and Economics
History, Literature, and Social Studies
Technology, Home Economics, or Livelihood subjects
I perform averagely across all subjects

questions.text: When given a difficult problem in school, how do you usually approach it?
options: I analyze it step-by-step using logic and formulas
I think about costs, benefits, and practical outcomes
I research, read, and discuss it with others
I look for a hands-on or practical way to solve it
I try different approaches depending on the situation

questions.text: Which type of school project excites you the most?
options: Science experiments or math competitions
Business simulations or financial planning activities
Debates, essays, or research papers on social issues
Building, cooking, designing, or technical demonstrations
Any project that doesn't require deep specialization

questions.text: How comfortable are you with numbers, equations, and data analysis?
options: Very comfortable — I enjoy working with numbers
Comfortable when applied to business or money matters
Not my strength, but I can manage basic computations
I prefer practical calculations (measurements, budgets, etc.)
It depends on the topic

questions.text: Which of these activities would you most likely do voluntarily?
options: Read science articles or solve math puzzles
Study market trends or watch business news
Read books, write stories, or follow current events
Learn a new skill like cooking, coding, or carpentry
Browse general topics without a specific focus

questions.text: In group work, what role do you naturally take?
options: The researcher who gathers data and analyzes findings
The planner who manages resources and sets the budget
The communicator who presents ideas and writes reports
The executor who builds, creates, or demonstrates the output
I adapt to whatever role is needed

questions.text: Which reading material would you most likely pick up?
options: A book about space, biology, or technology
A magazine about entrepreneurship or investing
A novel, a political commentary, or a philosophy book
A how-to guide or a technical manual
Whatever looks interesting at the moment

questions.text: How do you feel about conducting research and writing long reports?
options: I like it when it involves scientific or technical topics
I like it when it involves business cases or economic issues
I genuinely enjoy research and writing on any topic
I prefer shorter, more practical documentation
I can do it but it's not my favorite activity

questions.text: Which best describes your performance in Science subjects?
options: I consistently perform well and enjoy the subject
I do okay, but I'm more interested in how science applies to business
Science is not my strongest area; I prefer humanities
I prefer applied sciences like agriculture, electronics, or health
My performance is average across all science topics

questions.text: If you could attend a special seminar, which would you choose?
options: Robotics, astronomy, or medical science
Stock market investing or business management
Creative writing, human rights, or political science
Culinary arts, automotive, or computer hardware servicing
A general life skills or career orientation seminar

questions.text: Which career path interests you the most?
options: Doctor, engineer, scientist, or IT professional
Businessman, accountant, banker, or marketing professional
Lawyer, journalist, teacher, social worker, or politician
Chef, electrician, cosmetologist, farmer, or technician
I'm still undecided about my career

questions.text: Where do you see yourself working in the future?
options: In a laboratory, hospital, tech company, or research institution
In a corporate office, bank, or running my own business
In a school, government office, NGO, or media organization
In a workshop, salon, kitchen, farm, or technical facility
I'm not sure yet

questions.text: How important is it for you to pursue a college degree after SHS?
options: Very important — my dream career requires a specific college degree
Important — I plan to take a business or management course
Important — I want to take social sciences, law, or education
Not a priority — I want to work or start a business right after SHS
I'm still weighing my options

questions.text: Which of the following best describes your dream job?
options: A job that involves discovery, innovation, and solving complex problems
A job that involves managing people, money, and business operations
A job that involves influencing, educating, or advocating for others
A job that involves creating, building, or providing a skilled service
I don't have a specific dream job yet

questions.text: If you were to start a business, what kind would it be?
options: A tech startup, research company, or science-based enterprise
A trading business, investment firm, or management consultancy
A media outlet, school, social enterprise, or advocacy organization
A food business, repair shop, salon, or skilled services company
I haven't thought about starting a business

questions.text: How do you feel about the idea of working abroad in the future?
options: Yes — as a professional in engineering, medicine, or technology
Yes — in international business, finance, or trade
Yes — in diplomacy, education, or humanitarian work
Yes — as a skilled worker or through an exchange program
I prefer to work locally and am still undecided

questions.text: Which of these college courses interests you the most?
options: Engineering, Medicine, Computer Science, or Architecture
Accountancy, Business Administration, or Entrepreneurship
Political Science, Communications, Psychology, or Education
Vocational courses, Hospitality Management, or Agriculture
I haven't decided on a college course yet

questions.text: What does success look like to you after Senior High School?
options: Getting into a top university for a science or tech program
Starting a small business or landing a job in a company
Being accepted into a course in social sciences or arts
Having a job or livelihood skill that earns me stable income
Just finishing SHS and figuring things out from there

questions.text: How soon do you want to start earning money?
options: After college — I'm willing to study longer for a high-paying career
After college — but I may do part-time business while studying
After college — but the job's impact matters more than the pay
As soon as possible — I want to be financially independent after SHS
I haven't thought about this seriously yet

questions.text: What motivates you most about your future career?
options: Advancing human knowledge and solving big problems
Building wealth and achieving financial freedom
Creating positive change in society and helping communities
Mastering a craft and being self-reliant through a practical skill
Having a comfortable, stable life without too much pressure

questions.text: How do you prefer to learn new things?
options: Through experiments, data, and logical reasoning
Through case studies, simulations, and real-world business scenarios
Through reading, discussion, and exploring different perspectives
Through hands-on practice and learning by doing
Through a combination of methods depending on the topic

questions.text: Which best describes your thinking style?
options: Analytical — I like breaking down complex ideas into logical parts
Strategic — I think about what's most practical and profitable
Critical — I question norms and enjoy deep discussions on ideas
Concrete — I prefer clear, practical tasks with visible outcomes
Flexible — I adjust my thinking depending on the situation

questions.text: How do you handle pressure and difficult challenges?
options: I stay calm and rely on systematic problem-solving
I focus on finding the most efficient and cost-effective solution
I talk it out, seek opinions, and consider the human impact
I roll up my sleeves and take immediate, practical action
I tend to feel overwhelmed but push through eventually

questions.text: Which kind of environment do you prefer working in?
options: Quiet, structured, and focused (like a lab or study room)
Dynamic and goal-oriented (like an office or business setting)
Collaborative and expressive (like a classroom or community space)
Active and hands-on (like a workshop, kitchen, or field)
I can adapt to most environments

questions.text: How do you usually express yourself best?
options: Through graphs, formulas, experiments, or technical reports
Through presentations, proposals, or financial plans
Through writing, speaking, debates, or creative works
Through demonstrations, prototypes, or finished products
It depends on the situation

questions.text: When working on a long-term project, what is your biggest concern?
options: Making sure the data and methods are accurate and valid
Making sure it's profitable and well-organized
Making sure it advocates for something meaningful
Making sure the final output is functional and well-crafted
Making sure I can finish it without too much difficulty

questions.text: How do you feel about routine and repetitive tasks?
options: Fine, as long as the work involves precision and accuracy
Acceptable if it leads to measurable results and efficiency
I prefer variety — I enjoy exploring different ideas and issues
I prefer hands-on repetition that builds skill and mastery
I prefer a balanced mix of routine and new challenges

questions.text: Which of these words best describes your ideal self?
options: Innovative, logical, and scientifically minded
Ambitious, organized, and business-savvy
Empathetic, articulate, and socially aware
Skilled, resourceful, and practically capable
Balanced, adaptable, and open to many possibilities

questions.text: What is your family's general expectation for you after high school?
options: To pursue a prestigious college course like medicine or engineering
To take a business-related course or help in the family business
To pursue education, law, or public service
To learn a trade or find stable employment quickly
My family supports whatever I decide

questions.text: How would you describe your family's financial situation?
options: Comfortable — my family can fully support a long college education
Moderate — my family can support college but I need to be practical
Moderate — my family values education and will find a way to support it
Limited — I need to be able to work or earn as soon as possible
Varies — it's uncertain and I need to keep my options open

questions.text: Does your family have an existing business or livelihood you may continue?
options: No — my family works in professional or corporate careers
Yes — we have a business I may help manage or take over
No — my family is in government, education, or community work
Yes — we have a trade, farm, or small livelihood operation
Not applicable or I'm unsure

questions.text: How much does your family's opinion influence your strand choice?
options: A lot — they strongly encourage a specific career path
A lot — they want me to be financially successful
Somewhat — they support me but value my own interests too
Somewhat — they want me to be independent and self-sufficient
Very little — the decision is mostly mine to make

questions.text: Are you open to taking out a student loan or applying for scholarships?
options: Yes — I am willing to take that path for my dream career
Yes — especially if it leads to a high-earning career
Yes — if it allows me to pursue a meaningful profession
Preferably not — I'd rather take a path with lower financial risk
I haven't considered it yet

questions.text: How important is job security and a stable salary in your future career plans?
options: Important, but I prioritize passion and long-term growth
Very important — I want a high-paying and stable career
Somewhat important — meaning and impact matter more to me
Very important — I want to earn steadily as soon as possible
I haven't thought deeply about this yet

questions.text: Which of the following best matches your family's background or community?
options: My family highly values academic and scientific achievement
My family is involved in trade, commerce, or entrepreneurship
My family is active in education, politics, religion, or social causes
My family is involved in farming, craftsmanship, or skilled trades
My family background is mixed or doesn't strongly lean toward any of these
